zh.Of Whorlton In Cleveland, Yorkshire, Eng: 英國英格蘭越可爾爾頓
zh.lifeSummary: 當Joan Ros於大約1150年出生在英國越可爾時，她的父親Robert de Ros28歲，母親Sybil de Valognes24歲。她於1176年與Stephen de Meinill在英國英格蘭越可爾爾頓結婚。他們至少是3個兒子和1個女兒的父母。她去世在她的家鄉。

zh-hans.Of Whorlton In Cleveland, Yorkshire, Eng: 英国英格兰越可爾爾頓
zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Joan Ros于大约1150年出生在英国越可爾时，她的父亲Robert de Ros28岁，母亲Sybil de Valognes24岁。她于1176年与Stephen de Meinill在英国英格兰越可爾爾頓结婚。他们至少是3个儿子和1个女儿的父母。她去世在她的家乡。

nameMeanings.name: Ros
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Spanish Enrique, Luis, Jose, Alberto, Eduardo, Rafael, Elena, Fidel, Joaquin, Manuel, Pablo, Ramon.  Southeast Asian Leang, Phon, Lap, Phan, Pho, Saroeun, Sophal, Sophan, Sophea, Tha, Thang, Yen.
Catalan: nickname for someone with blond hair or a fair complexion, ultimately from Latin russus.

variants.name: Rus
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Romanian Simion, Grigore, Doina, Dorel, Ilie, Liviu, Petru, Vasile.

Czech, Slovak, and Romanian (of Slavic origin): ethnic name for a Russian, ultimately derived from an Old Norse term meaning ‘oarsman, rower’ (from the root roths-), a reference to the fact that the Russian state was established by Scandinavians who rowed their ships up rivers inland from the Baltic sea. In some cases this may also be origin of the Slovenian and Croatian surname (see 3 below).
Czech: descriptive nickname from the adjective rusý ‘blond’.

variants.name: Ross
meanings: Scottish: habitational name from one or other of a number of Scottish and English places called Ross or Roos(e), especially Roose (Lancashire) and Roos (East Yorkshire). The placenames derive from a British ancestor of Welsh rhos ‘moor, heath, plain’, which is the sense of the cognate Gaelic word ros. The Scottish surname has at least three origins. First, the Anglo-Norman family from Roos (East Yorkshire) was introduced to Scotland when Robert of Roos, lord of Wark Castle (Northumberland), married Isabella, an illegitimate daughter of King William the Lion. Second, various families took the name from the province of Ross in northern Scotland and other places of that name. Third, there were Norman families in Scotland by the 13th century who probably derived their name from Rots in Normandy (see 2 below). The descendants of Godfrey de Ros, tenant of the de Morville lords of Cunninghame, were major landholders in Ayrshire, and almost certainly took their name from Rots. The Rose family of Kilravock (Nairnshire) may take their name from either of these three (see Rose ). The lairds of Balnagown adopted the surname Ross after the earldom of Ross (to which they considered themselves rightful heirs) had passed into other hands through the female line.

English (of Norman origin): habitational name from Rots in Calvados (France), probably named with the ancient Germanic element rod ‘clearing’ (compare Rhodes ). This was the original home of a family de Ros, who were established in Kent in 1130 and had major estates in Kent until well into the 13th century.

English: habitational name from Wrose, in Shipley, near Bradford (Yorkshire), with re-spelling of Wr- as R- due to the loss of /w/ before /r/ in early modern English pronunciation. The spelling Wrose is no longer current. The placename derives from Old English wrāse ‘knot, something twisted’, referring to the steep-sided hill on which the settlement stands, with the sense ‘broken or twisting hill’.

nameMeanings.name: Joan
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

variants.name: John
meanings: Contracted form of Old French Jo(h)anne, from Latin Io(h)anna ( see Joanna ). In England this was the usual feminine form of John from the Middle English period onwards and was extremely popular, but in the 16th and 17th centuries it steadily lost ground to Jane . It was strongly revived in the first part of the 20th century, partly under the influence of George Bernard Shaw's play St Joan ( 1923 ), based on the life of Joan of Arc ( 1412–31 ). Claiming to be guided by the voices of the saints, she persuaded the French dauphin to defy the occupying English forces and have himself crowned, and she led the French army that raised the siege of Orleans in 1429 . The following year she was captured by the Burgundians and sold to the English, and a year later she was burned at the stake for witchcraft at the age of 18 or 19. Her story has captured the imagination of many writers, and she is variously portrayed as a national and political hero, a model of apolitical straightforwardness and honesty, and a religious heroine. She was canonized in 1920 . More recent influences have included the American film actress Joan Crawford ( 1908–77 , born Lucille le Sueur ), the British actress Joan Collins ( b. 1933 ), the American comedienne Joan Rivers ( b. 1933 ), and the West Indian pop singer Joan Armatrading ( b. 1950 ).

historicEvents.title: Magna Carta
historicEvents.description: King John of England agreed to a charter of rights called the Magna Carta. The document was originally drafted by the Archbishop of Canterbury to establish peace between King John and a group of rebel barons. The Magna Carta was completed in 1215 and is now considered to be one of the greatest constitutional documents of all time.

historicEvents.title: France Declares War on England
historicEvents.description: France declared war on England as Louis VIII of France attacked Poitou and northern Gascony.

historicEvents.title: 100 Years of Conflict Between England and France Ends
historicEvents.description: Treaty of Paris was signed on December 4, 1259, which ended 100 years of conflict between England and France.

en.lifeSummary: When Joan Ros was born about 1150, in Yorkshire, England, her father, Robert de Ros, was 28 and her mother, Sybil de Valognes, was 24. She married Stephen de Meinill in 1176, in Whorlton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom. They were the parents of at least 3 sons and 1 daughter. She died in her hometown.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Joan Ros est née environ 1150, à Everwyck, Angleterre, son père, Robert de Ros, avait 28 ans et sa mère, Sybil de Valognes, avait 24 ans. Elle a épousé Stephen de Meinill en 1176, à Whorlton, Everwyck, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Ils ont eu au moins 3 fils et 1 fille. Elle est décédée dans sa ville natale.

de.lifeSummary: Als Joan Ros um 1150 in Yorkshire, England geboren wurde, war ihr Vater Robert de Ros 28 und ihre Mutter Sybil de Valognes 24. Sie hat 1176 in Whorlton, Yorkshire, England, United Kingdom Stephen de Meinill geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 3 Söhne und 1 Tochter. Sie starb in ihrem Geburtsort.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Ros è nata il circa 1150 nel Yorkshire, England, sua padre, Robert de Ros, aveva 28 anni e sua madre, Sybil de Valognes, aveva 24 anni. Lei sposato Stephen de Meinill nel 1176, nel Whorlton, Yorkshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Erano genitori di almeno 3 figli e 1 figlia. Morì nel sua cittadina.

ja.lifeSummary: Joan Rosが約1150年にイギリスヨークシュアイアで生まれた時，父Robert de Rosは28歳，母Sybil de Valognesは24歳であった。1176年にイギリスイギリスヨークシュアイアウオルルトンでStephen de Meinillと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも3人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼女は故郷で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Joan Ros(이)가 대략 1150년 영국 요르크실르에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Robert de Ros(은)는 28세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Sybil de Valognes(은)는 24세였다. 그녀는 1176년 영국 잉글랜드 요르크실르 화를턴에서 Stephen de Meinill(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 세 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 그녀의 고향에서 사망했다.

mn.Of Whorlton In Cleveland, Yorkshire, Eng: Whorlton, Eoferwic, Англи, Нэгдсэн Вант Улс

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Joan Ros nasceu aproximadamente 1150, em Yorkshire, Inglaterra, seu pai, Robert de Ros, tinha 28 anos e sua mãe, Sybil de Valognes, tinha 24 anos. Ela casou-se com Stephen de Meinill em 1176, em Whorlton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Eles tiveram pelo menos 3 filhos e 1 filha. Ela faleceu em sua cidade natal.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Joan Ros родилась около 1150 г. в местности еркшир, Англия, её отцу по имени Robert de Ros было 28 лет, а матери, которую звали Sybil de Valognes, 24. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени Stephen de Meinill в 1176 г. в местности уаурлтон, еркшир, Англия, Великобритания. У них было по крайней мере 3 сына и 1 дочь. Она умерла в её родном городе.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Joan Ros nació aproximadamente 1150, en Yorkshire, Inglaterra, su papá, Robert de Ros, tenía 28 años y su mamá, Sybil de Valognes, tenía 24 años. Se casó con Stephen de Meinill en 1176, en Whorlton, Yorkshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Fueron padres de por lo menos 3 hijos y 1 hija. Murió en su pueblo.
